questions.question: Consider a coin flip with a biased coin that lands heads up 4/5 of the time. If the coin is flipped 10 times and it lands heads up 8 times, what is the probability that it was the biased coin? Use Bayes' theorem to find this conditional probability.

A medical test has a true positive rate of 90% and a false positive rate of 5%. If the test is administered to 1000 people, how many of them can be expected to be incorrectly diagnosed as having the disease?

Suppose we have a dataset of exam scores with a mean of 70 and a standard deviation of 10. If we receive a score of 85, what is the probability that it is at least 1 standard deviation above the mean? Use Bayes' theorem to find this conditional probability.
